_source: The Elements of Typographic Style, Interaction of Color by Josef Albers, Print Production standards
_note: Long-tail graphic design knowledge. Typography rules, color theory, grid systems, print specifications, file preparation.

serif.old_style: Garamond, Caslon; low contrast; angled stress; bracketed serifs; readable body text
serif.transitional: Baskerville, Times New Roman; higher contrast; vertical stress; workhorse fonts
serif.modern: Bodoni, Didot; extreme contrast; vertical stress; thin serifs; elegant, fashion
serif.slab_serif: Rockwell, Courier; thick serifs; minimal contrast; mechanical, typewriter feel

sans_serif.grotesque: Franklin Gothic; slight contrast; closed apertures; early sans serifs
sans_serif.neo_grotesque: Helvetica, Arial; minimal contrast; neutral; corporate, modern
sans_serif.humanist: Gill Sans, Frutiger; calligraphic influence; more organic; friendly
sans_serif.geometric: Futura, Avant Garde; based on circles/rectangles; modernist, clean

type_classification.script_display: Brush Script, Lobster; decorative; use sparingly; headlines only; avoid all caps

hierarchy_principles.size_contrast: Minimum 2:1 ratio; headline 36pt, subhead 18pt, body 9pt; create clear levels
hierarchy_principles.weight_contrast: Bold headlines, regular body, light captions; don't use more than 3 weights
hierarchy_principles.style_contrast: Italic for emphasis, all caps for short headlines; NEVER all caps body text (unreadable)
hierarchy_principles.color_contrast: Dark headlines, medium subheads, lighter captions; guide eye through hierarchy

readability_rules.line_length: 45-75 characters optimal (2-3 alphabets); 66 characters ideal; too long = lost line, too short = choppy
readability_rules.leading_line_height: 120-145% of point size; 12pt type = 14.4-17.4pt leading; more for long lines, less for short
readability_rules.tracking_letter_spacing: 0 for body text; slight positive (+10-25) for all caps; negative (-10 to -25) for large display
readability_rules.kerning: Adjust space between specific letter pairs; AV, To, We need negative kerning; UPPERCASE needs more space

alignment.left_aligned: Best for body text; ragged right; natural reading flow; Western languages
alignment.justified: Both edges aligned; professional; can create rivers (bad spacing); use with hyphenation
alignment.centered: Formal, invitations; hard to read for long text; use for short headlines only
alignment.right_aligned: Uncommon; captions, pull quotes; harder to read; use sparingly

rules.contrast: Pair serif with sans; geometric with humanist; create visual interest
rules.similar_proportions: Match x-height and width; ensures harmony even with contrast
rules.limit_fonts: 2-3 fonts max per project; use weights/sizes for variety within font family
rules.avoid_similar: Don't pair similar fonts (Helvetica + Arial); looks like mistake

proven_pairings: Playfair Display (serif display) + Source Sans Pro (sans body)

common_mistakes.rivers: Gaps in justified text forming vertical white space; adjust tracking or rewrite
common_mistakes.widows_orphans: Widow = single line at top of column; Orphan = single line at bottom; adjust tracking or rewrite
common_mistakes.rags: Uneven right edge in left-aligned text; manually adjust line breaks for smooth rag
common_mistakes.fake_bold_italic: Digitally slanting/thickening font; use actual font weights; fake versions look bad
common_mistakes.too_many_fonts: More than 3 fonts = chaotic; stick to one font family if unsure

color_models.rgb: Red, Green, Blue; additive; screens, digital; 0-255 per channel; sRGB for web
color_models.cmyk: Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, Black; subtractive; print; 0-100% per channel; converts from RGB for print
color_models.pantone_pms: Spot colors; consistent physical ink; brand colors; PMS 185 = Coca-Cola red

color_harmony.complementary: Opposite on color wheel; blue/orange, red/green; high contrast; vibrant
color_harmony.analogous: Adjacent colors; blue, blue-green, green; harmonious, calm; nature
color_harmony.triadic: Evenly spaced (120°); red, yellow, blue; balanced, vibrant; primary colors
color_harmony.split_complementary: Base + two adjacent to complement; blue, red-orange, yellow-orange; less tension than complementary
color_harmony.tetradic_square: Four colors evenly spaced; complex; use one dominant, others accents
color_harmony.monochromatic: Single hue with different saturation/brightness; sophisticated, minimal; easy to execute

60_30_10_rule.60_percent: Dominant color; backgrounds, large areas; neutral or primary brand color
60_30_10_rule.30_percent: Secondary color; supports dominant; creates interest
60_30_10_rule.10_percent: Accent color; calls to action, highlights; punchy, high contrast

psychological_associations.red: Energy, passion, danger, urgency; raises heart rate; CTA buttons, sale tags
psychological_associations.blue: Trust, calm, professional, stability; most universally liked; corporate, healthcare, finance
psychological_associations.green: Nature, growth, health, money; calming; organic products, environmental
psychological_associations.yellow: Optimism, warmth, caution; grabs attention; use sparingly (hard on eyes)
psychological_associations.purple: Luxury, creativity, wisdom, royalty; premium products, beauty
psychological_associations.orange: Friendly, energetic, affordable; attention-grabbing; CTAs, casual brands
psychological_associations.black: Sophisticated, powerful, elegant, modern; luxury brands, minimalism
psychological_associations.white: Clean, pure, simple, minimal; healthcare, tech, modern design

contrast_ratios.aa_large_text: 3:1 minimum; larger text easier to read at lower contrast
contrast_ratios.aaa_normal_text: 7:1 minimum; enhanced; better for vision impairment

accessibility_wcag.testing: WebAIM contrast checker; Figma plugins; ensure text readable for colorblind users
accessibility_wcag.dont_rely_on_color_alone: Use icons, patterns, or text labels in addition to color for meaning

grid_systems.column_grid: Divide page into vertical columns; 12-column common (divisible by 2, 3, 4, 6); flexible layout
grid_systems.modular_grid: Columns + rows; modules/cells; magazines, complex layouts; Swiss design
grid_systems.hierarchical_grid: Intuitive, organic; not strict columns; web design; based on content importance
grid_systems.baseline_grid: Horizontal lines for text alignment; 8pt or 4pt increments; aligns text across columns

margins_and_gutters.margins: Outer space; frame content; 0.5-1 inch print; 16-32px web; larger for elegance
margins_and_gutters.gutters: Space between columns; typically 20-30px web; 1-2 picas print; prevents column collision
margins_and_gutters.golden_ratio: 1:1.618; aesthetically pleasing proportions; margins, column widths, image sizes

white_space.purpose: Breathing room; guides eye; creates sophistication; don't fill every inch
white_space.macro: Space between major elements; sections, columns
white_space.micro: Space between smaller elements; line spacing, letter spacing, padding around text
white_space.active_vs_passive: Active directs attention; passive provides rest; balance both

visual_hierarchy.z_pattern: Eye moves Z-shape; top-left → top-right → bottom-left → bottom-right; simple layouts
visual_hierarchy.f_pattern: Eye scans top, then left side vertically; web reading; place important info in 'F' hotspots
visual_hierarchy.focal_point: Dominant element drawing attention; largest, highest contrast, or most colorful; one per layout

resolution_and_size.print_dpi: 300 DPI minimum; 450-600 DPI for fine art; lower DPI = pixelated print
resolution_and_size.screen_ppi: 72-96 PPI; higher wastes file size; 2× for retina displays (144-192 PPI)
resolution_and_size.vector_vs_raster: Vector (AI, PDF) = infinite scaling; Raster (JPG, PNG) = fixed resolution

bleed_and_safe_area.bleed: Extra image area beyond trim; 0.125" (1/8") standard; prevents white edges if cut misaligned
bleed_and_safe_area.safe_area: 0.125-0.25" inside trim; keep text/important elements inside; prevent accidental trimming
bleed_and_safe_area.trim_line: Final cut size; what customer receives
bleed_and_safe_area.setup: Design canvas = trim + bleed; 8.5×11 + 0.125" bleed = 8.75×11.25" canvas

color_mode_conversion.rgb_to_cmyk: Always convert before printing; colors shift (especially bright blues, greens); proof on calibrated monitor or physical proof
color_mode_conversion.out_of_gamut: Colors RGB can display but CMYK can't print; dulled in conversion; avoid neon colors for print
color_mode_conversion.rich_black: C:60 M:40 Y:40 K:100; deeper than K:100 alone; use for large black areas
color_mode_conversion.four_color_black_text: Never; causes registration issues; use K:100 only for text

file_formats.pdf_x1a: Print-ready PDF; CMYK only; fonts embedded; preferred by printers
file_formats.tiff: Raster; lossless; large files; compatible with all print workflows
file_formats.eps: Vector or raster; legacy format; being replaced by PDF
file_formats.ai_indd: Native Adobe; provide only if printer requests; include linked images and fonts (package for InDesign)

paper_stocks.coated: Glossy or matte coating; vibrant colors; magazines, brochures; ink sits on surface
paper_stocks.uncoated: Natural texture; ink absorbs; duller colors; letterhead, business cards, books

weight.cover_weight: 80-130 lb; thick, sturdy; postcards, business cards, book covers
weight.cardstock: 110-130 lb; very thick; invitations, premium cards

design_principles.contrast: Differentiate elements; size, color, weight, shape; creates interest and hierarchy
design_principles.repetition: Consistent use of colors, fonts, shapes; creates unity; brand recognition
design_principles.alignment: Nothing placed arbitrarily; everything connects; creates clean, organized appearance
design_principles.proximity: Related items grouped; creates relationships; whitespace separates unrelated items

balance.symmetrical: Mirror image; formal, stable; traditional, conservative
balance.asymmetrical: Unequal visual weight but balanced; dynamic, modern; more interesting
balance.radial: Elements radiate from center; movement, energy

design_principles.emphasis: Focal point; hierarchy; guides viewer's eye; one dominant element per composition
design_principles.unity: Cohesive whole; all elements work together; consistent style, color palette, spacing

before_sending_to_print: Check all images are 300 DPI minimum
Outline all fonts (convert text to paths) or embed fonts
Check for spot colors if unwanted (should be CMYK only unless intentional)
Ensure rich black used for large black areas (C:60 M:40 Y:40 K:100)
Set crop marks and bleed marks
Save as PDF/X-1a or printer's preferred format
Proof PDF on screen and request physical proof if critical
web_export: Convert to RGB color mode (sRGB color space)
Export at appropriate resolution (72-96 PPI standard; 144-192 for retina)
Optimize file size (compress JPG 70-85%; use PNG for transparency; WebP for modern browsers)
Ensure accessible color contrast (WCAG AA: 4.5:1 minimum)
Export assets at 1× and 2× for responsive images
